frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / race model
frameworks.one_line: decisions arise from competing accumulators racing to a bound

frameworks.name: neural population decoding
frameworks.one_line: logistic decoder reads choice accuracy from across-neuron variability

frameworks.name: decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: links pre-feedback belief in correctness to LIP accumulator population

core_question: Do neurons that represent evidence accumulation also carry information about choice accuracy (confidence)?
key_assumptions: decisions are implemented as races between competing evidence-accumulation processes
Tin neurons (response field on contralateral target) represent accumulation toward that target
confidence is the pre-feedback belief that one's choice is correct
manipulations: reaction-time random dot motion task with varying motion strength
Neuropixels recordings of LIP Tin-neuron population during contralateral choices
key_findings: shortly before a contralateral choice, the Tin population carries information about choice accuracy
average Tin pre-report activity is independent of reaction time and evidence strength
individual Tin neurons depart from the population mean in a consistent way
a simple logistic decoder on this variability predicts accuracy with the hallmarks of confidence
interpretations: across-neuron variability in the accumulator population, not the mean, encodes decision confidence
evidence-accumulation neurons jointly support choice and confidence representations
